channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Actor William Levy arrested after drunken 'disturbance' at Florida restaurant: report
item.description: Cuban-American actor William Levy was arrested in South Florida Monday after causing a "disturbance" inside a restaurant, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DOGE associate is made acting head of foreign assistance at the State Department, a US official says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has expanded the power of adviser Elon Musk's government-cutting team over the State Department, making a Musk lieutenant acting head of foreign assistance. A senior U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump assassination attempt suspect Ryan Routh smiles and laughs as attorneys spar over gun
item.description: President Donald Trump's alleged would-be assassin smiled and laughed during his Tuesday court hearing regarding the weapon he allegedly intended to use to shoot the president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gambian ex-soldier convicted at US trial of torturing suspected backers of a failed 2006 coup
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former member of Gambia's military was convicted Tuesday of charges that included torturing people suspected of involvement in a failed coup against the West African country's longtime dictator nearly 20 years ago.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Harvard stands to lose $2.2 billion in federal funding. Researchers fear science will suffer
item.description: In a high-stakes standoff, President Donald Trump's administration says it will freeze $2.2 billion in federal research grants for Harvard University, which is pushing back on demands for changes to campus policy.
item.dc:creator: BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Harvard's challenge to Trump administration could test limits of government power
item.description: On one side is Harvard, the nation’s oldest and wealthiest university, with a brand so powerful that its name is synonymous with prestige. On the other side is the Trump administration, determined to go further than any other White House to reshape American higher education.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Man who says he was held captive in Connecticut home for 20 years makes his first public comments
item.description: A man who told police that he was held captive in a Connecticut home for 20 years until being rescued in February says he recently celebrated his first-ever birthday party as he turned 32.
item.dc:creator: Dave Collins, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New England serial killer fears grow as police downplay threat encroaching on wealthy coastal enclaves
item.description: Internet sleuths are fueling speculation about a serial killer in New England after the discoveries of several sets of human remains across Connecticut, Rhode Island and Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Joe Biden will speak about Social Security in his return to the national stage
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Former President Joe Biden returns to the national stage Tuesday to elevate liberal concerns that President Donald Trump's agenda is threatening the health of Social Security.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Man allegedly held captive by stepmother releases first public statement
item.description: "I am speaking out today to begin the process of reclaiming my life and to have my say in how my story is told," he said.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia man arrested over alleged threats to kill Tulsi Gabbard
item.description: A Georgia man was arrested after federal authorities said he made numerous death threats against Director of National Intelligence (DNI) Tulsi Gabbard, the Justice Department announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NTSB finds 'failure' in training before deadly Newark cargo ship fire
item.description: The Newark fire department's lack of training and familiarity with marine firefighting during a deadly cargo ship fire at the East Coast's largest port in July 2023 amounted to a “failure of leadership,” the head of the National Transportation Safety Board said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida man confronts neighbor accused of shooting protected bird, video shows
item.description: A heated confrontation between a Florida homeowner and his neighbor over the shooting of a protected bird has prompted outrage in the community.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Trump not expected to attend White House Correspondents' dinner
item.description: President Donald Trump is not expected to attend next weekend's White House Correspondents' dinner, The Hill confirmed on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why every MLB player will wear the same number Tuesday
item.description: Every MLB player, regardless of team or position, will be wearing the exact same jersey number on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McDonald's teases return of Snack Wraps
item.description: McDonald’s may be one step closer to reintroducing its discontinued Snack Wraps, teasing a cryptic return date on social media Tuesday. On X, the company posted “snack wraps 0x.14.2025,” revealing the date the item will return but not the month.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Biden to rally support for Social Security in one of first major speeches since leaving office
item.description: Biden will be in Chicago on Tuesday to speak at the national conference of Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, KRON4

item.title: Elaine Wynn, Las Vegas casino co-founder, dies at 82
item.description: Elaine Wynn, a prominent philanthropist who co-founded a casino empire in Las Vegas with her ex-husband Steve Wynn, passed away at the age of 82, according to a release from the Elaine Wynn and Family Foundation.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: IRS tax deadline extended in multiple states: What to know
item.description: Residents in all or parts of 13 states can file for an extension to allow disaster-affected areas more time to submit their taxes.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, since the institution said Monday it won’t comply with the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Family of wrongly deported man calls for his return
item.description: White House officials and the leader of El Salvador are defending their decision to *not* return a Maryland man wrongly deported to El Salvador last month despite a Supreme Court ruling calling for the U.S. to facilitate his return.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, WGN 9

item.title: WATCH: College football trophy comes apart in Vance's hands at White House
item.description: The College Football Playoff National Championship Trophy came apart during a White House visit by the Ohio State Buckeyes football team Monday as Vice President JD Vance tried to pick it up.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: National Guard deployed to blue city overrun with juvenile crime, fentanyl is 'not enough,' state GOP says
item.description: After New Mexico's Democrat Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham announced she's sending National Guard personnel to Albuquerque, the state's GOP says the move isn't enough to help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prospective jurors excused from Weinstein retrial include an actor who played a Manhattan prosecutor
item.description: As jury selection begins in Harvey Weinstein’s New York City rape retrial, some prospective jurors made clear they couldn’t be fair in judging the one-time Hollywood mogul-turned-#MeToo pariah.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bill O'Reilly believes Trump has several tariff deals already done
item.description: Bill O'Reilly recently spoke with President Trump and believes the president has several tariff deals already done.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: FAA emergency order grounds NYC helicopter tour company involved in deadly crash
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal aviation regulators issued an emergency order Monday grounding the helicopter tour company involved in a deadly New York crash after learning it had fired its operations director minutes after he had agreed to suspend flights during the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Video shows mother pick up baby and run during Southern California earthquake
item.description: A quick-thinking San Diego County mother saved her baby from a 5.2 magnitude earthquake in eastern San Diego County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KTLA 5

item.title: Diddy pleads not guilty to additional charges in new indictment
item.description: The music mogul faces federal charges of racketeering, sex trafficking and of transportation to engage in prostitution.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KTLA 5

item.title: ICE, ATF capture fugitive in multinational manhunt wanted for 'brutal murder' in the Caribbean
item.description: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement announced the capture of a fugitive, wanted in connection to a 2020 murder, who escaped custody twice in the Caribbean.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro and the alleged arson at his official residence
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man is facing charges after authorities say he broke into the Pennsylvania governor’s mansion in the middle of the night, set a fire that left significant damage and forced Gov.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY and MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Meta faces historic antitrust trial that could force it to break off Instagram, WhatsApp
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. faces a historic antitrust trial beginning Monday that could force the tech giant to break off Instagram and WhatsApp, startups it bought more than a decade ago that have since grown into social media powerhouses.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY AP Technology Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Police say a person is in custody after a suspected arson fire at Pennsylvania governor's mansion
item.description: Police say a man has been arrested and will face charges including attempted murder, terrorism and attempted arson in the early morning fire that badly damaged the Pennsylvania governor's mansion and forced Gov. Josh Shapiro and his family to quickly escape.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The hierarchy behind the Hawaiian shirts at Trader Joe's
item.description: If you’re shopping at Trader Joe’s and you need the kind of assistance that only a seasoned, veteran TJ’s employee can provide, you might want to look at their shirts.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Trump sits cageside at Miami UFC event in his latest appearance at a sports event
item.description: Trump shook hands with some supporters as he walked to his cageside seat Saturday night at Miami’s Kaseya Center while others waved his trademark red campaign cap.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Intellectually disabled teen shot by Idaho police dies after being removed from life support
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An autistic, nonverbal teenage boy who was shot repeatedly by Idaho police from the other side of a chain-link fence while he was holding a knife died Saturday after being removed from life support, his family said.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE and REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 major unreleased movies we'll (probably) never get to see
item.description: "Coyote vs. Acme" was recently rescued from obscurity after being shelved by Warner Bros. Discovery. But other films haven't been so lucky.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Regulators order corrective action as Keystone Pipeline operators aim to restore service
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — Federal regulators have ordered the operator of the Keystone Pipeline to take several corrective actions after a rupture caused 147,000 gallons of oil to spill onto farmland in North Dakota, and the company said its goal is to resume deliveries to refineries on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
